name: Sluis (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the hoist panel and the tower
the waves and the church quarter

description: A red hoist panel with three white waves; the fly yellow, crossed by a blue diagonal band, bearing a red tower and, at the upper fly, a black church.
history: Flag of the municipality of Sluis in the west of Zeelandic Flanders, province of Zeeland; the quarters reflect its constituent former municipalities.

features.role: three white waves on the red hoist panel
a blue diagonal band across the fly
a red tower on the yellow field
a black church at the upper fly
